title: Weighted Mazes, Dijkstra, Heaps, and A* · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for weighted mazes, dijkstra, heaps, and a* and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, Tcl, Tk, display, input, assistive-technology, file, package, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for weighted Dijkstra and A-star comparison engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Define nonnegative passage costs, units, blocked edges, accumulated route cost, and the player meaning of weight.
Relax an edge only through a lower tentative cost and finalize vertices under the nonnegative-weight contract.
Push improved priorities and reject stale heap entries by comparing popped and recorded costs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Weighted Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Weighted Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz puzzle team handles case 4810 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate weighted relaxation, heap staleness, heuristic limits, route cost, and optimality; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare route validity, total cost, expansions, frontier peak, heuristic zero case, unreachable case, and changed weights.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare route validity, total cost, expansions, frontier peak, heuristic zero case, unreachable case, and changed weights.
